ShortFlowhttps://.github.io/Recent content on ShortFlowHugoen-usFri, 13 Mar 2026 00:00:00 +0900 Abouthttps://.github.io/about/Fri, 13 Mar 2026 00:00:00 +0900https://.github.io/about/

ShortFlow makes practical templates and prompt packs for YouTube Shorts creators.

The goal: help you publish more consistently—without reinventing your process every week.

  • Gumroad: https://masagc.gumroad.com
Why Your Shorts Die in the First 2 Seconds (and How to Fix It)https://.github.io/posts/why-your-shorts-die-in-the-first-2-seconds/Fri, 13 Mar 2026 00:00:00 +0900https://.github.io/posts/why-your-shorts-die-in-the-first-2-seconds/

Most Shorts fail for one reason: the opening doesn’t create enough curiosity momentum.

The 2-second rule

In the first 2 seconds, the viewer needs to feel one of these:

  • “This is about me.”
  • “I didn’t know that.”
  • “I want to see the result.”

If your first line is a generic intro (“Hey guys…”), you’re asking the viewer to wait. They won’t.

A simple hook formula

Use this structure:

Result + Time + Specific audience
